description: Baseline eval prompts. should_trigger entries must invoke the skill; should_not_trigger entries must not. Compatible with skill-creator's run_loop.py.

should_trigger.prompt: Describe our facial-recognition parking kiosk at the downtown garage as a DTPR datachain.
We're deploying a library self-checkout kiosk that scans library cards and photographs items. Make a DTPR datachain for it.
Document our 311 chatbot — it uses an LLM to triage citizen complaints and route them to a department. I need a transparency artifact.
Our HR team is piloting an AI resume screener. What would DTPR say about this system?
City contracted with a vendor that streams microphone data through a classifier to detect gunshots. I need to disclose how it works to the public.
I've attached a 12-page AIA PDF for our facial-recognition kiosk. Please describe it as a DTPR datachain.
Here's a URL to our company's AI policy — can you turn it into a DTPR datachain? https://example.com/ai-policy
I have a PDF of our resume-screener's spec and also want to verbally describe a post-launch observation about false negatives — combine both into a datachain.
I'm attaching a 60-page consultant report about our ShotSpotter-style audio classifier. Please describe it as a DTPR datachain.
This encrypted PDF describes our ML-based patient triage system. Please produce a DTPR datachain from it.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Help me refactor this TypeScript function to use Result types.
My GitHub Actions workflow is failing on the lint step. Help me debug it.
Write a SQL query to find the top 10 customers by order total in the last quarter.
DTPR doesn't have a good category for LLM hallucination disclosure — help me think through whether we should add one.
Can you grade the `accept_deny` element for public comprehension? I want to know if a commuter would understand it.
Audit the `ai__risks_mitigation` category for coherence — are the elements mutually exhaustive and non-overlapping?
